input.initialPrompt:
I have a clean macOS machine and want to start developing .NET MAUI apps
for iOS and Android. I can't run commands right now, but I need a complete
setup plan. Tell me exactly what I need to install, how to discover the
right versions, and how to verify everything works. Please provide the
official recommended installation sources.

data.content:
Here's a complete .NET MAUI setup plan for macOS targeting iOS and Android.

---

## 1. Install Xcode (iOS requirement)

- **Source:** Mac App Store or [developer.apple.com/xcode](https://developer.apple.com/xcode/)
- Install Xcode **Command Line Tools** after:
  ```
  xcode-select --install
  ```
- Open Xcode once to accept the license and install components.
- **Discover version:** MAUI docs specify the minimum Xcode version per .NET release. Check [learn.microsoft.com/dotnet/maui/whats-new](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/what-is-maui) release notes.

## 2. Install .NET SDK

- **Source:** [dot.net/download](https://dot.net/download) — get the latest **LTS** or **STS** release that supports MAUI.
- Use the macOS **arm64** or **x64** installer matching your Mac.
- **Verify:**
  ```
  dotnet --version
  dotnet --list-sdks
  ```

## 3. Install the MAUI Workload

```bash
sudo dotnet workload install maui
```
- **Discover available workloads:**
  ```
  dotnet workload search maui
  ```
- **Verify:**
  ```
  dotnet workload list
  ```
  You should see `maui` (or `maui-ios`, `maui-android`, etc.).

## 4. Install Java JDK (Android requirement)

- The MAUI workload installs **Microsoft OpenJDK** automatically. If not:
  - **Source:** [learn.microsoft.com/java/openjdk/download](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/java/openjdk/download)
  - MAUI typically requires **JDK 17**.
- **Verify:**
  ```
  java -version
  ```

## 5. Install Android SDK & Emulator

- The MAUI workload typically bootstraps Android SDK components. If you need manual control:
  - **Source:** [developer.android.com/studio](https://developer.android.com/studio) — install **Android Studio** or just the **Command-line tools**.
  - Required components: **Android SDK Platform**, **Build-Tools**, **Platform-Tools**, **Emulator**, and an **Android system image**.
- Accept Android licenses:
  ```
  dotnet build -t:InstallAndroidDependencies -f net9.0-android \
    -p:AndroidSdkDirectory="$HOME/Library/Android/sdk" \
    -p:AcceptAndroidSDKLicenses=true
  ```
- **Verify:**
  ```
  $HOME/Library/Android/sdk/tools/bin/sdkmanager --list
  ```

## 6. (Optional) Install an IDE

| IDE | Source |
|-----|--------|
| **Visual Studio Code** + C# Dev Kit + .NET MAUI extension | [code.visualstudio.com](https://code.visualstudio.com) |
| **JetBrains Rider** | [jetbrains.com/rider](https://www.jetbrains.com/rider/) |

> Visual Studio for Mac has been retired. VS Code or Rider are the recommended options on macOS.

## 7. Create and Run a Test Project

```bash
dotnet new maui -n HelloMaui
cd HelloMaui

# iOS (requires Xcode simulator)
dotnet build -f net9.0-ios
dotnet run -f net9.0-ios

# Android (requires emulator or connected device)
dotnet build -f net9.0-android
dotnet run -f net9.0-android
```

## 8. Full Verification Checklist

| Check | Command |
|-------|---------|
| .NET SDK installed | `dotnet --version` |
| MAUI workload present | `dotnet workload list` |
| JDK available | `java -version` |
| Xcode CLI tools | `xcode-select -p` |
| iOS simulators | `xcrun simctl list devices` |
| Android SDK | `dotnet build -f net9.0-android` succeeds |
| Template works | `dotnet new maui` creates project |

## Key Version-Discovery Strategy

Always cross-reference the [.NET MAUI release notes](https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/dotnet/maui/whats-new/dotnet-9) for your chosen .NET version — they list the **exact minimum Xcode, Android SDK, and JDK versions** required.
